reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly captures the informal statement. G is a finite group ([Group G] [Fintype G]), φ is a group automorphism (G ≃* G). The hypothesis uses a Finset I with (∀ x ∈ I, φ x = x⁻¹) and (3/4)·|G| < |I|; since I is a universally quantified parameter and the conclusion doesn't depend on it, this is equivalent to the existential "there exists such I" which in turn is equivalent to "|{x : φ(x) = x⁻¹}| > (3/4)|G|" — the informal condition. The conclusion is the conjunction of "φ(x) = x⁻¹ for all x" and "G is abelian (x*y = y*x for all x,y)", which exactly matches "φ(y) = y⁻¹ for all y ∈ G, and so G is abelian". The coercions between ℕ and ℚ in the inequality work correctly.
